title: Java first program
summary: Learn how a basic Java program is structured and how it prints text.
goal: By the end of this lesson, you will be able to read and write a very simple Java program that prints text to the console.
explanation: Java programs are usually organized inside classes. A class is a container that holds code.
The main method is the starting point of the program. When the program runs, Java begins there.
System.out.println() prints text to the console, similar to print() in Python or console.log() in JavaScript.
codeNotes: public class Main creates a class named Main.
main is the method Java runs first.
System.out.println() shows text in the console.
Each line ends with a semicolon because Java expects statements to end clearly.
commonMistakes: Forgetting a semicolon at the end of a line.
Using the wrong capitalization in System.out.println().
Missing a curly brace and breaking the class or method structure.

exercise.title: Print your own message
exercise.instructions: Write a Java program that prints your name on one line and your favorite language on the next line.

title: Java variables and if statements
summary: Learn how Java stores values in variables and checks a simple condition.
goal: By the end of this lesson, you will be able to create basic variables and use an if statement in Java.
explanation: A variable stores data that your program can use later. In Java, you often declare the type of data before the variable name.
For example, int stores whole numbers and String stores text.
An if statement checks whether a condition is true before running a block of code.
codeNotes: int completedLessons stores a whole number.
The if statement checks whether completedLessons is at least 3.
The + symbol joins text together in the printed sentence.
commonMistakes: Using the wrong data type for a value.
Forgetting parentheses around the if condition.
Leaving out curly braces around the code block.

exercise.title: Check learner access
exercise.instructions: Create a String variable for a learner name and an int variable for their score. Print a success message only if the score is 70 or higher.
